transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Good afternoon everyone. End of Sprint 4 Week 1. This has been a difficult week by any measure. I want a full honest account — what happened, where we are, and what the plan is for Week 2. Priya, start us off.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Okay, let me give the Week 1 summary. Monday and Tuesday we were blocked — staging environment was not functional due to an infra config issue. Vikram had done his part on Friday last week but the config was not applied by central infra until Monday 1:20pm. So effectively we lost Monday morning and Tuesday morning to follow-up and waiting.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: I want to be clear — the infra ticket was raised before Sprint 4 started. This was a dependency I flagged in Friday's weekly call. The delay was on central infra's side, not ours.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Noted. What was the downstream impact?

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Testing was delayed by 2 full days. I had 34 test cases ready from day 1, but with no environment I couldn't execute a single one until Monday afternoon. By end of week we've run 18 of 34. 18 passing. 16 still pending, mostly edge cases and the async fallback scenarios.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: On the development side — payment gateway endpoint has been verified. That was a concern early in the week, we were using the wrong URL initially. Suresh caught it Tuesday morning and corrected it. The core payment flow — initiation, success, failure handling — is all implemented and working on staging.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Timeout configuration is now set correctly at 10 seconds. We also added retry logic with proper user-facing feedback. Those were fixed mid-week. The one remaining backend item is the full gateway-down fallback — async queue, DB persistence, notification. That's still outstanding.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Frontend — payment confirmation page is done, field mapping is correct, mobile alignment is fixed. The error handling UI is improved. Main thing outstanding from my side is the retry state indicator — the 'processing, please wait' flow for slow connections.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: What happened with the Wednesday demo? I heard it didn't go well.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: The demo failed, sir. Client saw an error screen during the payment flow. The root cause was that the fix Suresh deployed was on staging-v2, but the demo was being run on staging-v1. The environments weren't in sync at that moment. It was a coordination gap — not a code issue — but client didn't see it that way.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: That's on me. I should have confirmed the demo environment before we let client in. We've fixed that now — Vikram set up a single deployment pipeline that syncs both staging environments simultaneously. Won't happen again.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Single pipeline is live since Wednesday noon. Anything deployed goes to both staging-v1 and staging-v2 at the same time. No more drift.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Client pushed the demo to Monday. That's the update I gave them. So Monday is now the new demo target. What's our confidence level for Monday?

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: If we complete the remaining 16 test cases over this weekend — and the prod SSL cert issue Vikram found yesterday gets resolved tonight — then Monday we can demo 30 of 34 cases with confidence. The 4 outstanding ones are the async gateway-down scenarios which are explicitly descoped.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Vikram — SSL cert status?

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Infra resolved it at 11:15pm last night. Prod SSL is now aligned. I confirmed it this morning. Neha started running the full suite on prod at midnight and went through till 1:30am.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Yes. Early results — 18 of 34 passing on prod, which matches staging. 2 of the remaining are intermittent — they sometimes pass, sometimes fail depending on gateway response time. I'll rerun this afternoon to build more data.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: For Monday's demo — we're showing the client the happy path and the main retry scenarios. We're transparent about the 2 intermittent cases and the descoped async queue. We frame it as Phase 1 go-live with documented limitations and a clear fix timeline.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: That's the right approach. What does Week 2 look like?

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Week 2 priorities — one, resolve the 2 intermittent prod failures permanently with the async queue. Two, get 34 out of 34 tests passing. Three, prepare for formal UAT which client wants by mid-week. We also have the Sprint 5 scope discussion coming which is getting complex.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: I'll handle the Sprint 5 scope discussion with client directly next week. You all focus on Week 2 delivery. Neha — I want 34 of 34 passing before end of next week. That's the target.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Understood sir. That's what I'm working toward.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: The async queue is 2-3 days of proper work. If we start Monday we can have it ready by Wednesday, which gives Neha Thursday-Friday for final test runs.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: That's the plan then. Okay team — difficult week, lessons learned. Let's make next week count. Good work staying on it. See you Monday.
